channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Flood Risk Doesn’t Matter as Bargain Houses Tempt NZ Buyers
item.description: New Zealand’s flood-prone homes are outperforming the rest of the country’s moribund housing market, as buyers chase lower prices and shrug off climate risks.
item.dc:creator: Ainsley Thomson

item.title: Floods in Southern China’s Guangxi Kill Two, Thousands Evacuated
item.description: Flooding triggered by Typhoon Maysak has sparked two deaths in southern China and forced thousands of people to evacuate, with more rains expected through mid-week, according to state media reports.
item.dc:creator: Foster Wong

item.title: Europe Faces Third Heat Wave as Fire and Power Risks Build
item.description: Western Europe is bracing for the summer’s third major heat wave, fueling dangerous wildfire conditions and straining power systems across the region.
item.dc:creator: Joe Wertz, Eva Brendel, Nayla Razzouk

item.title: Brookfield Approved as Lead Bidder, Lender to Polish Solar Firm
item.description: Private equity firm Brookfield Corp. won a key court ruling against funds managed by Joh. Berenberg, Gossler & Co. in the battle for control of bankrupt solar-power company GoldenPeaks Poland Holding Ltd.
item.dc:creator: Steven Church

item.title: Dogger Bank Wind Farm Challenges UK Approval of Nearby RWE Site
item.description: The owners of the world’s biggest offshore wind farm petitioned a UK judge to challenge the government’s decision to approve the development of another major project nearby, potentially throwing the new one into doubt.
item.dc:creator: Will Mathis

item.title: Heavy Rain Triggers Flood Watches in New York and Eastern US
item.description: Heavy rain will trigger flood watches from Ohio to southeastern Massachusetts, including New York City, and as far south as North Carolina, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: Climate Extremes Are Becoming More Extreme, Worrying Scientists
item.description: Heat, sea level rise, polar melting: They’re all picking up pace.
item.dc:creator: Eric Roston, Rachel Yeo

item.title: Teeth That Can Cut Cans: The Ravenous Fish Invading Greek Seas
item.description: Climate change is prompting the puffer fish to migrate from the tropical waters of the Indian Ocean to the Mediterranean.
item.dc:creator: Paul Tugwell

item.title: Even Scientists Are Shocked by Record-Breaking Extreme Heat
item.description: Record-breaking heat waves are even shocking scientists, who have long braced for climate change. Olivia Rudgard explains why. (Source: Bloomberg)
item.dc:creator: Autor não encontrado

item.title: Extreme Heat Isn't the Only Climate Impact Shocking Scientists
item.description: For the full experience visit: Extreme Heat Isn't the Only Climate Impact Shocking Scientists
item.dc:creator: Hayley Warren, Eric Roston
